page: Microsoft Azure FX48-24mds_v2 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the FXmdsv2 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 24 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 12 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8573C processor with an x86_64 architecture. The CPU operates at a base frequency of 4.2 GHz and includes a 260 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 1008.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 42.0 GB per physical core. The instance includes 2835 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable. No GPUs are included in this configuration.

In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates strong single-core and memory performance. The Geekbench single-core score of 2339.0 and text processing score of 2938.0 both rank in the top tier (top 10%). Single-threaded gzip compression (34.71 GB/sec) and bzip3 decompression (14.24 GB/sec) are also strong, placing in the top 25%. Large-block read-only memory bandwidth reaches 186.82 GB/sec, which is in the top 25% tier. Multi-core performance is strong with a Geekbench multi-core score of 16414.0, while database operations and Redis SET operations perform in the average tier.

The hardware profile represents a high-density memory configuration paired with high-frequency processing cores. This makes the server highly cost-efficient for workloads that require massive memory footprints but do not scale infinitely across hundreds of parallel threads, avoiding the overhead of paying for excess vCPUs. Tradeoffs include the lack of GPU acceleration and a moderate physical core count relative to the massive memory size. This server is best suited for memory-intensive databases, large-scale text processing, caching layers, and enterprise applications requiring high single-core performance alongside massive RAM capacity.
description: Microsoft Azure FX48-24mds_v2 is a memory-optimized virtual machine powered by a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 24 dedicated vCPUs (12 physical cores) and 1008.0 GB of RAM. Running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this x86_64 instance includes 2835 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, though it lacks GPU acceleration. Benchmarks highlight top-tier single-core performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2339.0 and text processing score of 2938.0 placing in the top 10%. Large-block memory read bandwidth is strong at 186.82 GB/sec. This high memory-to-core ratio of 42.0 GB per core makes the instance highly efficient for memory-bound workloads. It is optimized for large-scale databases, in-memory caching, text processing, and enterprise applications requiring high single-thread performance and massive memory capacity.
og_description: Microsoft Azure FX48-24mds_v2 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 24 vCPUs, 1008 GB RAM, and 2835 GB local storage, delivering top-tier single-core performance for memory-intensive workloads.

tagline: A high-frequency, memory-optimized virtual machine featuring massive RAM capacity and top-tier single-core performance for demanding database and text-processing workloads.

1008.0 GB of RAM providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 42.0 GB per core
